balance_focal_point_bank_v1.description: Modular balance, visual weight, and focal point observation fragments. Designed for short, confident, conversational styling feedback that feels intuitive—not analytical.

positive_balance: The visual weight feels evenly distributed.
There’s a clear focal point, which works well.
Nothing is overpowering the rest of the look.
The elements feel balanced.
The accessories don’t compete with the outfit.
There’s a strong center of attention here.
The statement piece is doing its job.
The look feels anchored.
The details are supporting each other nicely.
It feels cohesive from top to bottom.
Everything feels placed on purpose.
The look feels balanced without feeling boring.
Your eye moves through the outfit smoothly.
The outfit feels styled, not cluttered.
The focus is clear and it reads confident.
The outfit feels balanced—nothing is overpowering.
The visual weight is distributed really nicely.
It feels styled but still clean and easy.
Your eye knows exactly where to land.
The details feel coordinated, not competing.
This has a strong presence without feeling loud.
Everything feels intentional and placed on purpose.
The look feels grounded and confident.
The accessories support the outfit instead of stealing it.
This reads cohesive from head to toe.
positive_focal_point: The jacket naturally draws the eye.
The shoes anchor the look well.
The bag adds just enough emphasis.
The neckline creates a nice focus.
The silhouette itself becomes the statement.
The earrings give the look a strong finishing touch.
The color placement creates a nice focal point.
The belt detail pulls the outfit together.
The layering creates a clean point of interest.
The texture adds a subtle statement without overpowering it.
The outer layer is acting as the hero piece.
The shoe choice anchors the outfit beautifully.
The bag is giving the look direction.
The neckline creates a strong visual center.
The texture contrast adds quiet emphasis.
The contrast detail is doing the spotlight work.
The color blocking creates a clear visual anchor.
The proportions themselves become the statement.
The accessory placement feels intentional and controlled.
The structure is naturally pulling the focus together.
constructive_competing_elements: There isn’t a clear focal point yet.
The accessories are slightly competing.
Too many elements are asking for attention.
The eye doesn’t know where to land.
The statement piece gets a bit lost.
There’s more visual weight at the bottom.
The upper half feels heavier than the lower half.
The bold pieces are competing with each other.
It needs one hero element.
The look feels slightly scattered.
It’s a little busy in one area.
The focus keeps shifting, so it doesn’t feel settled.
One detail is overpowering the rest of the outfit.
The outfit needs a clearer main character piece.
It’s close—just slightly over-styled right now.
The eye keeps bouncing instead of settling.
The statement element is competing with another detail.
There’s slightly too much happening at once.
The focal point isn’t clearly defined yet.
The visual emphasis feels split between pieces.
The bold elements are overlapping in energy.
One zone is carrying more weight than the rest.
The outfit feels a touch visually crowded.
The emphasis is a bit diffused right now.
It needs one cleaner decision to settle the look.
refinement_suggestions: Removing one accessory would simplify it.
Letting one piece stand out would strengthen it.
Switching to subtler accessories would help.
Choosing one statement element would sharpen it.
Balancing the top and bottom visually would elevate it.
Reducing one bold detail would clean it up.
Adding a grounding piece could anchor it.
Keeping everything else minimal would let this shine.
Softening one strong element would create harmony.
Introducing a subtle layer could add structure.
Pick one hero piece and keep the rest quiet.
If you simplify the accessories, the outfit will look more premium.
A cleaner bag or shoe choice would reduce visual noise.
Keeping either the top or bottom simple will make it feel more balanced.
If you remove one competing detail, the whole look will click.
Choose one focal point and let the rest support it.
If you quiet one strong element, everything will harmonize better.
A single anchor piece (bag/shoe/jacket) will make it feel complete.
If you simplify one side of the outfit, the balance improves instantly.
Reducing visual clutter will make it feel more intentional.

usage_rules.default_pattern: Use 1 balance or focal observation. Add 1 refinement only when improvement is requested or implied.
avoid: Stacking multiple critiques in a short response.
Sounding overly analytical unless the user asks for a deeper breakdown.
usage_rules.tone_integration: Fragments must be wrapped inside AHVI's response structure (Reaction → Observation → Optional refinement → Invitation). Keep it natural and confident.
